known_issues: SOR horizontal banding in lower face/neck

physical_lens_pipeline.dual_surface_refraction_corrected: NOT NEEDED — solver uses thin lens formula (n-1) which already accounts for both refractive surfaces. Audit: AUDIT_dual_refraction.md
physical_lens_pipeline.correction_factor: none (1.0) — solver's (n-1) formula is the dual-surface plano-convex thin lens equation
physical_lens_pipeline.source_image_uncertainty: RESOLVED — v1 PROD woodblock mesh (b845296) used INKFORGE/woodblock.png (457KB grayscale, MD5 a08bb8...). V2 used woodblock2.png (1.39MB RGB, MD5 c7ff32...). Different source images confirmed. See VERIFICATION_woodblock_source.md.

charcol_24in_standard.dual_refraction_applied: inherent in solver — no post-correction needed

charcol_24in_deep.dual_refraction_applied: inherent in solver — no post-correction needed

example_8in.dual_refraction_applied: inherent in solver — no post-correction needed

woodblock_24in_standard.dual_refraction_applied: inherent in solver — no post-correction needed
woodblock_24in_standard.solver_params_at_build: artifactSize=0.1m, focalLength=0.75m (30" throw) — STALE CONFIG, predates 24"/48" defaults

woodblock_8in.dual_refraction_applied: inherent in solver — no post-correction needed
woodblock_8in.solver_params_at_build: artifactSize=0.1m, focalLength=0.75m (30" throw) — STALE CONFIG, predates 24"/48" defaults

woodblock_8in.key_finding: 1/4 bit overshoots mesh resolution (3.2 cells/step). 1/8 matches (1.6 cells/step). 1/16+ finer than mesh — no gain.

bit_resolution_analysis.size_dependent_rule: 8in → 1/8 finish (cell=0.0078"). 24in → 1/4 finish (cell=0.023"). Bit stepover should be ≤ 2x mesh cell spacing.

blockers.description: FALSE ALARM. Solver uses (n-1) thin lens formula which IS the dual-surface plano-convex equation. Both refractive surfaces are accounted for. Ray tracer also applies Snell's law twice (entry + exit). No correction needed. See AUDIT_dual_refraction.md.

CONFIRMED: V1 PROD woodblock mesh used INKFORGE/woodblock.png (457KB, MD5 a08bb8...). V2 used woodblock2.png (1.39MB, MD5 c7ff32...). Different images (r=0.907). The stale run.log in Final cows/woodblock/normal/ refers to an earlier 512px run, not the current 1024px PROD mesh.

V1 woodblock PROD mesh (b845296) was built with artifactSize=0.1m, focalLength=0.75m (30" throw). The project now targets 24"/48" config. This mesh cannot be used for 48" installations without re-running the solver.

v1_v2_comparison.refraction_equivalence_note: Both yield slope = delta/(f*(n-1)) in paraxial limit. Depth difference is from different physical configs, not physics errors.
v1_v2_comparison.recommended_cut: V2 8" at 48" throw — purpose-designed for target installation, G-code ready. V1 woodblock uses stale 30" throw config.
v1_v2_comparison.v2_gcode_status: ready (roughing + spiral finishing + square cut, CAUSTICFORGE v2.0, 1/4" ball nose)
v1_v2_comparison.v2_gcode_concern: 128px mesh resolution — 1/4" ball 20% stepover is finer than mesh cells (0.0625"). Should run at 256px+ before production.

comparison_8in_24throw.recommendation: V1 1024px for immediate CNC cut. V2 needs more OT iterations at higher resolutions.

next_session_scope: Review COMPARISON_v1_v2_8in_24throw.md. V1 1024px is CNC-ready. V2 edge_r degrades at higher res — needs more iterations or GPU. Key decisions: (1) Cut V1 8in proof? (2) Tune V2 convergence?
